title: podman run: Failed to write input to service
body:
### Issue Description

When running `podman run -ti` (e.g. `podman run -ti --rm alpine:3.19 /bin/sh`) fairly often (more than 50% of the time) I see an error message like `ERRO[0021] Failed to write input to service: EOF` and I don't get a shell I can use.

The container seems to start OK since I can use `podman exec` to start another process in the container, and inspect things.

```
❯ podman exec -ti b9d81f814dce /bin/sh
/ # ps aux
PID   USER     TIME  COMMAND
    1 root      0:00 /bin/sh
    2 root      0:00 /bin/sh
    3 root      0:00 ps aux
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install latest podman desktop on MacOS machine
2.  Install podman + podman machine via the desktop app
3. Run `podman run -ti --rm alpine:3.19 /bin/sh` and observe output:

```
podman run -ti --rm alpine:3.19 /bin/sh
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:3.19...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:c303524923177661067f7eb378c3dd5277088c2676ebd1cd78e68397bb80fdbf
Copying config sha256:1dc785547989b0db1c3cd9949c57574393e69bea98bfe044b0588e24721aa402
Writing manifest to image destination

ERRO[0021] Failed to write input to service: EOF
```

4. Exec into container to confirm it seems to have started OK:

```
❯ podman exec -ti b9d81f814dce /bin/sh
/ # ps aux
PID   USER     TIME  COMMAND
    1 root      0:00 /bin/sh
    2 root      0:00 /bin/sh
    3 root      0:00 ps aux
```


### Describe the results you received

Error message like this:

```
ERRO[0021] Failed to write input to service: EOF
```

or this:

```
HTTP/1.1 204 No Content
Api-Version: 1.41
Libpod-Api-Version: 4.8.3
Server: Libpod/4.8.3 (linux)
X-Reference-Id: 0x4001326010
Date: Fri, 19 Jan 2024 11:55:26 GMT
```

And then things hang, I cannot get the container to exit with `Ctrl-C`

### Describe the results you expected

To see a shell

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

MacOS 13.6.3

### Additional information

The shell on my host machine is zsh.

I have noticed that this error is unlikely to occur when I open a fresh window / tab in my terminal emulator, but almost always occurs when using a window that has been open for a while.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> Does this still happen?

Yip, just today.
